marketFocus: Iran-Krieg Woche 3 — Hezbollah koordinierter Angriff: IRGC 40. Angriffswelle auf Tel Aviv/Jerusalem/Haifa. Hezbollah + Iran führten ersten koordinierten Angriff auf Nordisrael seit Kriegsbeginn durch. Bahrain Fuel Tanks angegriffen. Irak Ölhäfen komplett gestoppt. WTI settled $87.25/bbl (+$3.80), Brent $91.98/bbl. IEA 400mln bbl Emergency Release angekündigt — Markt ignoriert es als 'Band-Aid'. Trump: 'practically nothing left to target, any time I want it to end, it will end' — aber Axios: mindestens 2 weitere Wochen Strikes geplant.

US CPI In-Line, PCE-Komponenten Hot — Fed Cuts auf Dezember verschoben: US CPI Feb 2.4% YoY (in-line), Core CPI 2.4% YoY (in-line). PCE-Komponenten lehnten hot — Pantheon Macroeconomics erwartet Core PCE Deflator bei 0.4% im Februar. Money Markets: Fed rate cut erst Dezember fully priced (von 2 auf 1 Cut). DXY stärker, Yields höher. USD bleibt haven-gestützt und durch sticky inflation untermauert.

RBA Hike — 4 Banken jetzt im Hike-Camp: ANZ + Goldman Sachs + NAB + Westpac erwarten alle RBA Hike nächste Woche. AUD/USD outperformed alle G10 Peers — Range 0.7113-0.7185. ASX 200 fiel trotzdem (Öl-Inflation dämpft Sentiment). Heute: BoE Governor Bailey Speech (Zeit TBD) — entscheidend ob GBP zurück in Bullish dreht. US Unemployment Claims 13:30 Frankfurt.

USD.summary: USD strengthened as DXY tracked yields higher amid rising oil prices and sticky CPI. US CPI Feb came in exactly in-line (2.4% YoY headline, 2.4% Core) but the PCE components leaned hot — Pantheon Macroeconomics looks for Core PCE deflator to remain at 0.4% in February. Money markets pushed back Fed rate cut pricing to December (from earlier expectations of 2 cuts). USTR Greer announced Section 301 investigations into 16 trading partners including China, EU, Mexico — adding a trade war risk premium to USD. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: DXY strengthened — yields higher, Fed cuts pushed to December
US CPI Feb 2.4% YoY (in-line), PCE components hot — stagflation narrative intact
USTR Section 301 investigation: 16 trading partners (China, EU, Mexico, Japan)
Iran war: 5,500+ targets struck, 60+ ships — no end in sight despite Trump rhetoric
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment

EUR.summary: EUR/USD traded within a 1.1577-1.1645 range — pressured by a firmer USD and ongoing energy disruptions. ECB speakers sent mixed signals: Kazimir said a rate hike on Iran may be closer than thought but no reason to act at next meeting; Villeroy said no hike at next week's ECB meeting; Nagel said ECB will act decisively if energy spike feeds into durably higher inflation; Schnabel said monetary policy is in a good place and they must monitor the persistence of the energy price shock. EU warned Iran conflict could push Eurozone inflation above 3%. EUR is trapped between firmer front-end rate pricing and a deteriorating growth outlook. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: EUR/USD 1.1577-1.1645 range — pressured by firmer USD and energy disruptions
ECB divided: Kazimir (hike closer), Villeroy (no hike), Nagel (act if persistent), Schnabel (good place)
EU warns Iran conflict could push Eurozone inflation above 3%
USTR Section 301 investigation into EU — trade war risk adds to EUR headwinds
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment

GBP.summary: GBP marginally weakened but just about retained the 1.3400 handle. UK Chancellor Reeves warned there will be dislocation in the labour market and a response is needed — a cautious signal on UK growth outlook. UK RICS House Price Balance Feb came in at -12% (exp. -9%, prev. -10%) — a miss, adding to the soft UK data picture. The dominant driver of GBP's recent strength — BoE easing priced out — is being tested. BoE Governor Bailey speaks today: if he sounds relaxed about inflation, the gilt yield support could fade quickly and GBP could turn Bearish. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Neutral = no High Conviction alignment.
drivers: GBP marginally weakened, held 1.3400 — cyclical currencies weighed by risk-off
UK RICS House Price Balance Feb -12% (exp. -9%) — miss, soft UK data
UK Chancellor Reeves: 'labour market dislocation, response needed'
BoE Governor Bailey speaks today — hawkish = GBP bid, dovish = GBP offered
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Neutral = no High Conviction alignment

JPY.summary: USD/JPY edged higher to return to the 159.00 handle as the firmer USD and higher US yields added to the headwinds for Japan. BoJ Governor Ueda said foreign exchange is an important factor affecting the economy and prices, and noted the need to be mindful that forex has larger impacts on prices than in the past. However, Ueda's comments were not hawkish enough to support JPY — the BoJ remains accommodative while Japan faces a severe energy import shock. Higher oil prices are a direct negative for Japan's terms of trade (90%+ crude from Middle East). Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: USD/JPY back to 159.00 — firmer USD, higher US yields, oil pressure
BoJ Ueda: FX important factor, mindful of larger price impacts — but not hawkish
Japan 90%+ crude from Middle East — severe terms-of-trade shock
Higher oil prices = direct JPY negative (energy importer)
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment

AUD.summary: AUD extended outperformance as ANZ and Goldman Sachs joined NAB and Westpac in calling for an RBA rate hike at next week's meeting. AUD/USD traded towards the upper end of a 0.7113-0.7185 range. The oil-related inflation pressures have spurred further hawkish central bank calls — Westpac previously forecast a May hike but now believes the RBA will be 'compelled to react' to the recent strength in oil prices. ASX 200 fell on risk-off mood but AUD FX outperformed. Australia temporarily permitted higher sulphur levels in fuel as an emergency measure. AUD is Daily Bullish but Weekly Neutral — strong intraday case but no High Conviction alignment.
drivers: AUD/USD 0.7113-0.7185 — extended outperformance vs all G10
4 banks now expect RBA hike next week: ANZ, Goldman Sachs, NAB, Westpac
Westpac: RBA 'compelled to react' to oil price strength — previously forecast May
Australia temporarily permits higher sulphur fuel — emergency energy response
Weekly Neutral + Daily Bullish = no High Conviction alignment (strong intraday)

CAD.summary: WTI settled $87.25/bbl (+$3.80) — still significantly elevated, keeping CAD's petro-currency advantage intact. Canada's Energy Minister said the government is working urgently with industry to assess the possible pace and scale of action on oil production — a supply-side positive that adds to CAD's structural advantage. The IEA 400mln bbl release announcement is a potential mild CAD headwind if oil falls further, but the level remains highly supportive. OPEC MOMR maintained 2026 world oil demand forecast at 1.4mln BPD — no downgrade. Weekly Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: WTI $87.25/bbl (+$3.80) — elevated, CAD petro-advantage intact
Canada Energy Minister: working urgently on oil production boost — supply positive
IEA 400mln bbl release: potential mild headwind if oil falls, but level still supportive
OPEC MOMR: 2026 oil demand forecast maintained at 1.4mln BPD — no downgrade
Weekly Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment

CHF.summary: CHF mildly underperformed alongside EUR and JPY as net oil importers faced headwinds from rising crude prices. USD/JPY and EUR/USD moves dominated FX while CHF traded quietly. The SNB cap at EUR/CHF 0.9000 remains in place. Spot gold declined amid a firmer USD and as oil fluctuations muddled the inflation and interest rate outlooks. CHF's haven role is being partially offset by its energy import exposure. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Neutral = no High Conviction alignment.
drivers: CHF mildly underperformed — oil importer headwind alongside EUR and JPY
Spot gold declined on firmer USD — reduces CHF haven premium
SNB cap at EUR/CHF 0.9000 — limits CHF appreciation
Oil fluctuations muddle inflation/rate outlook — CHF direction unclear
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Neutral = no High Conviction alignment

NZD.summary: Antipodeans were subdued owing to their high-beta characteristics and pressure in metal prices. NZD underperformed AUD — the RBA hike story is AUD-specific and NZD lacks its own catalyst. Copper futures retreated with risk sentiment spooked by surging oil prices and the US Section 301 investigation announcement into 16 trading partners including China. Bitcoin declined below $70,000 — a broader risk-off signal. RBNZ remains dovish with no domestic rate hike catalyst. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: Antipodeans subdued — high-beta pressure, metal prices declined
NZD underperformed AUD — RBA hike story is AUD-specific, NZD lacks catalyst
Copper retreated — US Section 301 investigation into China adds risk
Bitcoin below $70,000 — broader risk-off signal
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment

bonds.name: US 10Y Treasury
bonds.summary: T-notes were lower as yields rose across the curve amid rising oil prices, likely dealer concession ahead of the 10-year auction, and with some hot-leaning PCE components in the CPI report. Money markets now only fully price one Fed rate cut this year, not until December. The 3-year auction on Tuesday was weak — demand concerns at the front end. The 10-year auction today will be closely watched for demand signals.
drivers: T-notes lower — yields higher on oil prices, dealer concession, hot PCE components
Fed rate cut: only 1 cut fully priced for 2026, not until December
3yr auction Tuesday was weak — demand concerns at front end
10yr auction today — key demand signal for Treasuries
US CPI in-line but PCE components hot — stagflation narrative intact

bonds.name: German Bund 10Y
bonds.summary: Bund futures fell beneath the 126.00 level after Wednesday's one-way downward price action, as oil prices and geopolitics continued to dominate markets. ECB speakers (Kazimir, Kazaks) added to the bearish fixed income tone — odds of an ECB hike by end-2026 fully priced. The 2036 Bund tap was weak (b/c 1.18x vs prev. 1.46x, yield 2.89% vs prev. 2.73%). EU warned Iran conflict could push Eurozone inflation above 3%.
drivers: Bund futures below 126.00 — oil prices + ECB hawkish repricing
ECB Kazimir: hike closer than thought — odds of 2026 hike fully priced
2036 Bund tap weak: b/c 1.18x (prev. 1.46x), yield 2.89% (prev. 2.73%)
EU: Iran conflict could push Eurozone inflation above 3%
One-way downward price action Wednesday — bearish momentum

bonds.name: JGB 10Y
bonds.summary: 10yr JGB futures lacked demand amid oil-related inflation pressures, while there was little reaction to comments from BoJ Governor Ueda. Ueda noted that they need to be mindful that FX has larger impacts on prices than in the past and could affect inflation expectations. JGBs face competing forces: oil-driven inflation pressure (bearish) vs delayed BoJ normalisation (bullish). The BoJ remains accommodative — no imminent hike signal.
drivers: JGB futures lacked demand — oil inflation pressures weigh
BoJ Ueda: mindful FX has larger price impacts, will conduct appropriate policy
BoJ remains accommodative — no imminent hike signal
Oil-driven inflation (bearish JGB) vs delayed BoJ normalisation (bullish JGB)
Japan 90%+ crude from Middle East — terms-of-trade shock ongoing
